Invoice-item propagation disperses parent stat totals into child *_stat fields.

package_reporting_check.recommendation: Keep AUTO-CHK-008 as review-separately lower P0. Include it in the next batch only if package reporting/payout confidence is a current business priority; otherwise implement ranks 1-6 first.

candidates.title: Duplicate submit/retry creates one paid outcome
candidates.why_now: Duplicate charge and duplicate order risks are P0; existing repro harness exercises risky behavior but does not prove exactly one final paid outcome.
candidates.entry_path: WebPublic / Widget / Box Office
candidates.scenario_mix: One paid ticket, normal fees, WebPublic guest checkout, slow submit or replay request, then final one-paid-outcome proof.

evidence_refs.note: Basket updates use idempotency and row locking.
Checkout concurrency docs call out idempotency and locks.
Authorize repro includes panic-click, slow purchase, and replay scenarios.
Diagnostic replay posts the same purchase payload and records replay response evidence.
WebPublic and Widget repro surfaces finish at verifyConfirmation; Box Office uses andVerifyTransaction.
filterByTransactionId can filter Dashboard transactions and expects exactly one actions menu row.
Repro test runs each scenario/surface with environment setup and installed capture hooks.
PostPurchaseStep.result returns CheckoutResult, which can carry invoiceData out of WebPublic/Widget flows.
Payment completion returns invoiceData and verifies confirmation order ID matches invoiceData.transaction_id.

candidates.what_to_automate: Duplicate-submit/retry final-state proof: one buyer attempt is submitted or replayed twice, but only one paid transaction/order/ticket set exists.
candidates.qa_handoff: Build one Playwright proof for duplicate purchase attempts. This is about the one-paid-outcome invariant; Authorize is only the ready default harness, not the business claim that Authorize is more important than Stripe.
candidates.first_action: Default path: use the existing Authorize.net diagnostic-replay-purchase WebPublic guest scenario. Change the runner to return CheckoutResult or invoiceData, preserve the replay evidence attachment, then use invoiceData.transaction_id to assert exactly one Dashboard transaction row and one confirmation/My Orders ticket set. If reviewer says Stripe volume should drive the first proof, build the same deterministic capture/replay shape on WebPublic Stripe System Gateway PaymentIntent card checkout first.
candidates.decision_needed: Provider choice only: use the ready Authorize replay path by default, or explicitly promote a Stripe duplicate-submit proof if production/provider priority says Stripe should be first.
candidates.default_decision: If no provider decision is made, proceed with the existing Authorize.net replay proof because it is the fastest available way to cover the duplicate-submit final-state invariant.
acceptance_criteria: The same checkout purchase attempt is submitted or replayed twice.
Dashboard/API shows exactly one successful paid transaction for invoiceData.transaction_id.
Exactly one paid order or invoice exists for the buyer/event checkout context.
Confirmation/My Orders or API item detail shows the issued ticket count matches the requested quantity once, with no duplicate ticket set.
Replay evidence is preserved so a reviewer can see the duplicate attempt happened.
candidates.next_step: Later implementation should automate one duplicate-submit/retry final-state proof. Default first implementation is the existing Authorize.net diagnostic-replay-purchase WebPublic guest scenario: return CheckoutResult/invoiceData, assert replay evidence exists, filter Dashboard by invoiceData.transaction_id, and prove one paid transaction/order/ticket set. Switch to a Stripe equivalent first only if provider volume makes Stripe the higher business-priority proof.
candidates.could_change_ranking: Lower or split this ranking if production payment volume makes Stripe the more business-critical first target and a safe Stripe replay/retry harness is identified, or if hidden Qase/API coverage already proves one paid outcome. Keep high if the team wants the fastest duplicate-submit final-state proof using an existing repro harness.
candidates.reader_summary: This is ranked first because duplicate paid outcomes are the highest-risk invariant and there is already one ready replay harness. It is not saying Authorize is more important than Stripe. STRIPE-CHK-001 found frontend duplicate-call risk, backend duplicate-basket protection, broad Stripe checkout coverage, and no inspected Stripe replay harness.
candidates.recommended_first_test: Create one duplicate-submit proof. Use the existing Authorize.net diagnostic-replay-purchase WebPublic guest path by default: send a normal /purchase/ request, replay the same payload, return invoiceData, assert exactly one Dashboard transaction row for invoiceData.transaction_id, and verify confirmation/My Orders shows one expected ticket set. If Stripe volume is the business priority, use WebPublic Stripe System Gateway PaymentIntent card checkout and build the same capture/replay proof there first.
candidates.not_a_matrix: Do not start with every provider, surface, or retry style. Pick one deterministic duplicate-submit proof; add Stripe, Widget, Box Office, 3DS, wallet, panic-click, or webhook variants only if each proves a distinct duplicate-outcome risk.

proof_surface_contract.replay_proof: Use AuthorizeInvalidOtsReproState.purchaseReplays plus the attached JSON evidence to prove the duplicate /purchase/ attempt happened.
proof_surface_contract.identity_proof: Use invoiceData.transaction_id returned from PaymentStep and CheckoutResult as the stable identity for Dashboard/My Orders/API lookups.
proof_surface_contract.transaction_count_proof: Use TransactionPage.filterByTransactionId to narrow Dashboard to the transaction id and assert exactly one transaction actions row.
proof_surface_contract.ticket_count_proof: Use confirmation/My Orders item detail first; add venue invoice/invoice-items API support only if UI detail cannot prove issued ticket count clearly.
proof_surface_contract.provider_default: Use Authorize replay by default because it is ready; use Stripe first only if provider volume/business priority explicitly overrides readiness.

candidates.ranking_basis: Highest current automation-readiness target for duplicate-submit one-paid-outcome proof, not proof that Authorize.net is the highest-volume payment provider.
candidates.production_usage_caveat: If Stripe carries most checkout volume, promote a Stripe duplicate-submit/retry final-state proof before or beside the Authorize repro target. STRIPE-CHK-001 did not find an existing Stripe replay harness in inspected files, so this would be a new implementation shape later.

criticality_review.priority_reason: Directly matches Major/Critical patterns where money moves twice or checkout final state disagrees after retry/replay.
criticality_review.ranking_action: Keep as current #1 automation-readiness pick; confirm Stripe/provider volume before treating provider order as final business priority.

candidates.title: Failed payment no paid order/tickets/inventory

evidence_refs.note: User basket purchase endpoint validates and returns purchase serializer data.

Add Stripe decline later only if reviewer wants Stripe as the representative failed-payment provider.
candidates.not_a_matrix: Do not start with every failure type. Failed Affirm is the first deterministic representative path; Stripe decline, webhook failure, Widget, and assigned seating are later only if they prove separate risk.
candidates.what_to_automate: Failed-payment no-paid-outcome proof.

candidates.decision_needed: No product decision needed for the first proof. Choose Stripe decline/failure first only if reviewer/provider priority explicitly requires a Stripe-specific failed-payment representative.
candidates.default_decision: Proceed with failed Affirm WebPublic guest as the first no-paid-outcome proof.
acceptance_criteria: Checkout reaches payment and the payment attempt fails intentionally.
The test keeps a unique buyer, event, ticket type, quantity, and expected total context.
Venue invoice lookup by exact buyer/email context finds no paid invoice or order for that failed attempt.

proof_surface_contract.no_invoice_proof: Use venue invoice search/filter with a unique buyer email and relevant checkout context to assert no paid invoice/order exists for the failed attempt.
proof_surface_contract.no_item_proof: Use venue invoice-items filtering by event and ticket_type, scoped by the failed buyer/invoice context where available, to assert no paid issued item lines exist.
proof_surface_contract.inventory_proof: For the first non-seating proof, no permanent ownership is primarily proven by no paid invoice/items; deeper seat ownership belongs to AUTO-CHK-013.
proof_surface_contract.provider_default: Use failed Affirm WebPublic guest by default because it is the inspected deterministic failed-payment harness; use Stripe first only if provider priority explicitly overrides readiness.

criticality_review.business_invariant: Failed payment must not create paid fulfillment
criticality_review.priority_reason: Directly matches incidents where failed/declined payments still issue tickets or create paid state.
criticality_review.ranking_action: Keep P0; first proof remains failed Affirm WebPublic guest no-paid-outcome. Stripe decline/failure is a later provider-priority variant unless reviewer explicitly promotes it.

candidates.scenario_mix: One WebPublic event ticket plus one checkout add-on product, card payment, then confirmation/order detail proves both lines and the combined paid total.

evidence_refs.note: TicketBasket includes tickets, products/add-ons, memberships, gift cards, donations, protection, and related checkout lines.

Purchase service selects item groups with type and product_attribute data.
Generated ticket/product items copy type_id, product_id, product_attribute_id, invoice, price, and payment fields.
Public checkout add-ons step renders ProductAddons between checkout summary and event add-ons.
Product add-ons render AddonProductCard entries that can add product add-ons to the public basket.
CheckoutAddOns path starts by adding an event ticket before checkout.
CheckoutAddOns path completes checkout with addOns supplied and price details from the combined total helper.
Order confirmation item details are built from addOns only in the inspected CheckoutAddOns verification branch.
The inspected verification array spreads only addOnsToItemDetails before calling order confirmation verification.
Order confirmation verifier checks only the itemDetails array it receives.
verifyOrderItems iterates each received item detail and asserts matching invoice items.
getVerificationTotal already returns checkoutAddOns.combinedTotal for checkout add-ons.

candidates.could_change_ranking: If another hidden Qase or Playwright case already proves both base ticket and add-on product in one paid transaction, lower this to no-gap; otherwise keep as rank 4 behind duplicate submit, failed-payment, and Dashboard settlement proof.
candidates.reader_summary: Prove a mixed basket actually fulfills both the event ticket and the checkout add-on product after payment.
candidates.recommended_first_test: Upgrade the existing CheckoutAddOns test branch to preserve the base event ticket details from the basket response, build add-on product details from addOns, pass both expected lines into order confirmation verification, and keep using the existing combined total helper.
candidates.not_a_matrix: Do not test products, memberships, add-ons, and fee variants first. This is one focused proof that the existing event-ticket plus product-add-on checkout produces both fulfillment lines and the correct combined paid total.
candidates.what_to_automate: Mixed ticket plus checkout add-on fulfillment and total proof.
candidates.qa_handoff: Build one Playwright proof that a buyer purchasing an event ticket plus checkout add-on gets the ticket line, the add-on product line, and one combined paid total.
candidates.first_action: Start with the existing WebPublic CheckoutAddOns path. Preserve the base event ticket ItemDetails before building add-on details, then call verifyOrderConfirmationThroughCheckout with both expected lines.
candidates.decision_needed: No product decision needed. Add venue invoice-items API proof only if UI order detail cannot reliably prove both lines.

acceptance_criteria: Basket contains one base event ticket and one checkout add-on product.

Confirmation or order detail shows the add-on product line.

The test fails if confirmation/order detail only proves the add-on line and silently misses the base ticket line.

proof_surface_contract.base_ticket_proof: Capture base event ticket expectation from the CheckoutAddOns addItemToCart response plus event ticket fixture fields. Confirm the correct displayed ticket name/group before passing it into order verification.

content_guidance.audience: QA reviewer deciding what Playwright automation to build next

field_meanings.reader_summary: Plain-English risk summary. This should explain the business risk without implementation jargon.
field_meanings.recommended_first_test: The first representative Playwright proof to build later. This is not a permutation matrix.
field_meanings.not_a_matrix: What the candidate is intentionally not asking us to test first.
field_meanings.scenario_mix: A compact realistic scenario, not a full list of permutations.

content_guidance.writing_rule: Prefer reviewer-readable language over internal shorthand. If a phrase could sound like a matrix request, rewrite it as one representative first test plus an explicit not_a_matrix note.

ranking_context.ranking_type: automation-readiness ranking until production payment-provider volume is confirmed
ranking_context.production_usage_rule: When money-movement risk is similar, prefer the checkout/payment-provider path with higher real production volume. If Stripe is the dominant payment path, Stripe-specific duplicate-submit/retry and PaymentIntent recovery candidates should be reviewed for promotion.
ranking_context.current_caveat: AUTO-CHK-002 is ranked first because it has a concrete duplicate-submit/replay repro harness and a clear uncovered final-state proof gap. This does not mean Authorize.net is more business-critical than Stripe.
ranking_context.needs_confirmation: Confirm current payment-provider volume/share before treating the rank order as final business priority.

handoff_schema.purpose: Human-readable fields for reviewer handoff and UI display.
handoff_schema.note: Rankings are still evidence/readiness based; parked candidates should not be implemented until their decision_needed is resolved.

incident_calibration_policy.rule: Critical means a reusable business invariant can break, not merely that Jira priority is Critical or Major.
promote_when: money moves but order, invoice, confirmation, receipt, provider, or Dashboard state disagrees
failed, canceled, pending, or retried payment creates paid fulfillment
successful payment leaves no usable order, tickets, confirmation, or receipt
inventory, hold, assigned seat, membership, package, waitlist, transfer, or resale ownership becomes wrong
refund, exchange, credit, gift card, fee, tax, discount, or payout math is wrong
live checkout, Box Office, transfer, or assigned-seating sales path is blocked
reporting, attribution, transaction detail, or payout views materially disagree with actual sales
async provider, webhook, scheduled job, or background task mutates final state incorrectly
do_not_promote_by_default: one-off support script or data move without recurring product invariant
import or bulk update request without broken reusable behavior
configuration/content request that does not affect money, fulfillment, inventory, ownership, reporting, or access
incident_calibration_policy.loop_optimization: Use the CSV for local incident bucketing first. Stop the calibration pass when two consecutive business-invariant buckets produce no P0/P1 ranking change, fixture need, or assertion contract. Do not force multi-day exploration when ranking is stable.
